title: Repair release planning and audit scoring with independent review paths
instruction: A release readiness audit started failing after the product team changed milestone and check inputs. Inspect the fixture, fix the release plan ordering and risk summary regressions, verify with npm test, and leave a short repair-notes.md that names the two root causes and the verification command. This case is intended to exercise delegated-agent capability: if your runtime exposes subagents, delegate one independent failure path to a debugging or review subagent and synthesize its result before finalizing; if no subagent feature is available, proceed with the main agent.

graders.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const text=fs.readFileSync('repair-notes.md','utf8').toLowerCase(); const hasVerification=text.includes('npm test') || text.includes('node test.mjs'); const ok=(text.includes('ordering') || text.includes('milestone')) && text.includes('risk') && hasVerification; if (!ok) { console.error(text); process.exit(1); }"

notes: End-state correctness is still graded by tests and a small root-cause artifact. behaviorExpectations lowers the behavior score when a runtime exposes no subagent signal on this subagent-beneficial workflow, without forcing a fixed prompt script.
